entries.notes: The fundamental move, often the hardest because it requires habit change.

entries.description: From informal local description to documented local schema.
entries.workRequired: Define and document a local schema with required and optional fields.
entries.notes: The point at which the work becomes shareable within a team.

entries.description: From local schema to informal or emerging shared schema.
entries.workRequired: Adopt or align with a schema used beyond the project.
entries.notes: May involve participation in emerging community efforts.

entries.description: From emerging to accepted shared schema.
entries.workRequired: Adopt or align with a community-endorsed schema.
entries.notes: May require remapping of existing records.

entries.resourcesAffectedNotes: R13, plus vocabulary-controlled fields in other resources.
entries.workRequired: Map local terms to AAT, Wikidata, domain thesauri.
entries.notes: Often happens incrementally, field by field.

entries.fromNotes: T03 or T04 (a or b) or T05.
entries.workRequired: Mint and register PIDs for resources and entities.
entries.notes: Often happens independently of T05, can be parallel.

entries.description: Enriching PID-bearing metadata with optional context.
entries.workRequired: Populate optional fields, link related resources at metadata level.
entries.notes: A legitimate plateau, many projects will stay here.

entries.workRequired: Apply CIDOC CRM or extensions, possibly without recommended templates.
entries.notes: Common but limited route, may need rework to reach T07b.

entries.workRequired: Apply recommended templates, document modelling choices.
entries.notes: The strategic route, supports automated lifts.

entries.description: Aligning ad hoc semantic representation to recommended implementations.
entries.workRequired: Refactor existing semantic data to match recommended templates.
entries.notes: May be substantial work, depends on how far ad hoc representations diverged.

entries.workRequired: Publish metadata as linked data, register with aggregators.
entries.notes: Metadata-level federation, no full semantic layer required.

entries.workRequired: Publish semantic representations as resolvable linked data.

entries.description: Assembling resources into a documented Digital Twin.
entries.workRequired: Specify the Digital Twin structure, assemble resources accordingly.
entries.notes: Depends on Digital Twin specification work.

entries.description: Exporting from a higher level to a lower level for exchange with partners working at that level.
entries.workRequired: Generate simpler representations from richer ones.
entries.notes: The bidirectional flow that distinguishes the framework from a one-way stack.

entries.description: Accessibility improvement: moving a resource from restricted to documented or open access at any level.
entries.workRequired: Storage migration, licensing review, access policy work.
entries.notes: Cuts across all levels, often the bottleneck. Closely related to but distinct from X17 (licensing).

entries.description: Scope expansion: moving a resource from individual to project or institutional scope.
entries.notes: Particularly relevant at T02 and T03.

entries.description: Licensing improvement: applying or clarifying re-use licences for a resource at any level. Moves from no licence stated, through bespoke terms, towards machine-readable open licences. A FAIR-R consideration that applies at every level from T02 upward.
entries.workRequired: Review of re-use intentions, selection of appropriate licence (Creative Commons, Open Data Commons, or domain-specific), application to records and metadata, registration of licence in relevant infrastructure (repository deposits, PID metadata, aggregator records).
entries.notes: Often treated together with accessibility but conceptually distinct. A resource can be openly accessible without a clear re-use licence, leaving re-users in a legal grey area, or can be access-restricted with a clear licence that applies once access is granted. Becomes increasingly important at T04b and above where aggregator and federation infrastructures typically require explicit licensing.
